questions.question: Which breed is the most common backyard chicken?
questions.funFact: Rhode Island Reds are hardy and friendly and can lay up to 300 brown eggs per year.

questions.question: What breed has fluffy feathers that look like fur?
questions.funFact: Silkies have a unique gene that prevents their feathers from interlocking giving them a silky soft feel.

questions.question: Which breed is famous for being very large and calm?
questions.funFact: Brahma chickens can weigh up to 5.5kg and were once called the King of Chickens.

questions.question: What is a Bantam chicken known for?
questions.funFact: Bantams are typically one-fifth to one-quarter the size of standard chickens.

questions.question: Which breed lays blue or green eggs?
questions.funFact: Araucana chickens originated in Chile and carry a gene that deposits pigment into the eggshell.

questions.question: Which breed holds the world record for most eggs in a year?
questions.funFact: An ISA Brown hen once laid 365 eggs in 364 days — almost one every single day.

questions.question: What breed is known for black feathers and Australian origin?
questions.funFact: The Australorp set the world egg-laying record in 1922 with 364 eggs in 365 days.

questions.question: Which ancient breed is considered one of the oldest domesticated chickens?
questions.funFact: The Red Jungle Fowl is the wild ancestor of all domestic chickens first domesticated around 7000 BC.

questions.question: What is a Frizzle chicken known for?
questions.funFact: Frizzle feathers curl outward and upward due to a single dominant gene mutation.

questions.question: Which dual-purpose breed was developed in the USA in the 1800s and named after a state?
questions.funFact: Rhode Island Red was developed in Massachusetts and Rhode Island and became the official state bird of Rhode Island.

questions.question: How many days does a chicken egg take to hatch?
questions.funFact: The embryo turns itself over 50 times per day inside the egg during incubation.

questions.question: What colours do most common chickens lay?
questions.funFact: Shell colour is determined entirely by breed genetics not diet or environment.

questions.question: What do you call keeping a fertilised egg warm to hatch?
questions.funFact: The ideal incubation temperature is 37.5°C — even 1°C off can affect hatch rates.

questions.question: Which part of the egg protects the developing chick?
questions.funFact: An eggshell has up to 17000 tiny pores that allow oxygen in and carbon dioxide out.

questions.question: What determines the colour of a chicken's eggshell?
questions.funFact: You can predict a hen's egg colour by looking at her earlobes — red earlobes usually mean brown eggs.

questions.question: At what age do most hens begin laying eggs?
questions.funFact: This first lay is called point of lay and varies by breed — some take up to 32 weeks.

questions.question: What is the small white spot on a yolk called?
questions.funFact: The blastodisc is where fertilisation occurs — if fertilised it becomes the blastoderm.

questions.question: How many eggs can a productive hen lay per year?
questions.funFact: Production drops by around 10-15% each year after the first laying season.

questions.question: What string-like structure keeps the yolk centred in the egg?
questions.funFact: The chalaza is a twisted strand of egg white that anchors the yolk — thicker means a fresher egg.

questions.question: Which nutrient in egg yolk is essential for brain development?
questions.funFact: One egg yolk contains about 147mg of choline — roughly 27% of the recommended daily intake.

questions.question: What is the main ingredient in standard chicken feed?
questions.funFact: Corn provides energy while soybean meal provides the protein chickens need to lay eggs.

questions.question: How often should chickens have access to fresh water?
questions.funFact: A laying hen drinks about 500ml of water per day — more in hot weather.

questions.question: What common food is DANGEROUS for chickens?
questions.funFact: Avocado contains persin a fungicidal toxin that causes heart failure in birds.

questions.question: What nutrient do chickens need most for strong eggshells?
questions.funFact: Crushed oyster shells are the most popular calcium supplement for laying hens.

questions.question: What is grit used for in chicken digestion?
questions.funFact: Chickens have no teeth — they swallow grit which helps their muscular gizzard grind up food.

questions.question: How much feed does an average laying hen eat per day?
questions.funFact: Free-range hens eat less commercial feed because they supplement with insects and grass.

questions.question: Which kitchen scrap should never be fed to chickens?
questions.funFact: Raw potato skins contain solanine a toxic glycoalkaloid that can harm chickens.

questions.question: What feed type is best for chicks in their first weeks?
questions.funFact: Starter crumble has 20-24% protein — layer pellets have only 16% and too much calcium for chicks.

questions.question: What mineral deficiency causes soft or thin eggshells?
questions.funFact: Hens require 4g of calcium per day for eggshell production — deficiency causes shell-less eggs.

questions.question: Why do some keepers add apple cider vinegar to chicken water?
questions.funFact: Apple cider vinegar lowers water pH making it inhospitable to harmful bacteria and promoting good gut flora.

questions.question: What is the most visible sign a chicken is unwell?
questions.funFact: Healthy chickens are alert and active — a sick hen will separate herself from the flock.

questions.question: What are mites and lice on chickens classified as?
questions.funFact: Red mites feed on chickens at night and hide in coop cracks during the day.

questions.question: What should you do if a chicken sneezes frequently?
questions.funFact: Frequent sneezing can indicate respiratory infection — early isolation prevents flock spread.

questions.question: What is Marek's Disease?
questions.funFact: Marek's Disease is caused by a herpesvirus and can be prevented with vaccination at hatch.

questions.question: What condition causes a hen's abdomen to swell with fluid?
questions.funFact: Ascites is caused by heart or liver failure leading to fluid accumulation in the abdomen.

questions.question: What is bumblefoot in chickens?
questions.funFact: Bumblefoot is caused by Staphylococcus bacteria entering through cuts — rough perches are a common cause.

questions.question: What does egg binding mean?
options: A hen lays too many eggs
A hen cannot pass an egg
questions.funFact: A hen with egg binding can die within 48 hours — warm baths and calcium supplements can help.

questions.question: What parasite causes coccidiosis in young chicks?
questions.funFact: Coccidiosis kills more young chicks than almost any other disease — wet litter accelerates its spread.

questions.question: Which disease causes tumours on nerves and organs of chickens?
questions.funFact: Marek's Disease can also cause paralysis of legs and wings and eye tumours leading to blindness.

questions.question: What is the correct term for inflammation of a hen's oviduct?
questions.funFact: Salpingitis causes the oviduct to fill with a caseous mass called a lash egg — a serious condition.

questions.question: How do chickens communicate with each other?
questions.funFact: Hens use a specific cluck to call chicks to food and a different alarm call for aerial vs ground predators.

questions.question: What is a male chicken called?
questions.funFact: A male under 1 year is a cockerel — over 1 year he becomes a rooster.

questions.question: Approximately how many chickens are there in the world?
questions.funFact: There are more chickens on Earth than any other bird — roughly 4.5 chickens per human.

questions.question: What is a group of chickens called?
questions.funFact: A group of chicks specifically is called a brood and a group with a rooster is called a peep.

questions.question: Can chickens recognise human faces?
questions.funFact: Chickens can distinguish between familiar and unfamiliar humans and remember faces long-term.

questions.question: What is the red fleshy growth on top of a chicken's head?
questions.funFact: The comb helps regulate body temperature — blood circulates through it to release heat.

questions.question: How fast can a chicken run at full speed?
questions.funFact: Chickens can also fly short distances — up to 3 metres high and 50 metres distance.

questions.question: What is the average lifespan of a backyard chicken?
questions.funFact: The oldest recorded chicken Muffy lived to 23 years old in the USA.

questions.question: What is the scientific name for the domestic chicken?
questions.funFact: Gallus gallus domesticus descended from the Red Jungle Fowl Gallus gallus native to Southeast Asia.

questions.question: Which country has the highest chicken-to-human ratio?
questions.funFact: Guyana has approximately 4.4 chickens per person driven by its large poultry farming industry.
